name: Vaul (Unstyled Drawer Component for React)

performance.notes: CSS transforms with spring physics; 60fps on mobile

agent_readiness.notes: Unstyled drawer (bottom sheet) component built on Radix Dialog. Drawer.Root, Drawer.Trigger, Drawer.Portal, Drawer.Content. Spring physics drag interaction. Snap points for partial-open states. Direction: top/bottom/left/right. Works with shadcn/ui. By Emil Kowalski (also creator of Sonner). ~5KB. Used in shadcn/ui component library. Mobile-first but works on desktop. Accessible via Radix primitives.

gotchas: Snap points API requires careful calculation
Direction other than bottom is less polished
Not a full mobile sheet implementation (no velocity gestures)
best_when: Mobile-first React apps needing bottom sheet drawers
shadcn/ui projects wanting the standard drawer component
Progressive disclosure patterns (partial open → full open)
avoid_when: Complex native mobile-like sheet gestures (native iOS/Android SDKs better)
summary: Vaul is the go-to unstyled drawer/bottom sheet for React, built on Radix Dialog with spring physics drag interactions. Drawer.Root/Trigger/Portal/Content. Snap points for partial-open states. Integrated into shadcn/ui. ~5KB. By Emil Kowalski (Sonner creator). Headless — bring your own styles. Best for mobile-first React apps needing accessible bottom sheets with natural drag behavior.
